projectsPage.heading: My Projects
projectsPage.intro: Automation tools, web applications, and system utilities

projects.title: Synapse
projects.summary: Cross-platform health companion for managing medications, symptoms, appointments, recovery, and personal health details in one place
projects.tags: Expo SDK 54, React Native, TypeScript, Supabase, Expo Router, EAS Build
projects.overview: Synapse is a mobile health management app built with Expo and React Native. It helps users track scheduled and PRN medications, log symptoms, manage appointments, store health profile details, and use iOS widgets for quick access.
projects.features:
Scheduled and PRN medication management
Dose logging, reminder flows, refill data, and pharmacy details
Symptom, daily log, and recovery tracking
Appointment scheduling with doctor notes and visit prep
Native iOS widgets plus committed iOS and Android project support
projects.contributionIntro: Built a production-minded health companion with a structured Expo Router app, native iOS support, Supabase-backed infrastructure, and app-store-ready release work.
projects.contributions:
Organized the active app around the synapse-reset source of truth
Added medication, symptom, appointment, report, and recovery flows
Built widget sync support for hydration, sick mode, and mental health experiences
Maintained iOS/Android native folders, EAS configuration, and production-safe config fallbacks

projects.title: LifeOS
projects.summary: Personal operating system for choosing priorities, protecting focus, planning days, capturing thoughts, and organizing life into spaces
projects.tags: Next.js, React 19, TypeScript, Firebase, Framer Motion, Tauri
projects.overview: LifeOS is a modular productivity cockpit built as a Next.js app with native macOS packaging through Tauri. It combines tasks, calendar, focus sessions, spaces, brain capture, settings, and integrations into one calm command center.
projects.features:
Now, Today, Spaces, Library, Settings, Tasks, Calendar, Notes, Brain, Knowledge, and Resources views
Focus mode with session tracking, halfway break prompts, floating resume dock, and keyboard shortcuts
Calendar events with Google, iCloud, and import workflows
Brain capture and command palette for fast task and thought entry
Context Radar browser extension that detects approved job or learning sites without reading sensitive fields
projects.contributionIntro: Designed and built an ADHD-friendly personal operating system that turns scattered work into visible priorities, focus sessions, calendar structure, and searchable personal context.
projects.contributions:
Built a pluggable module architecture for tasks, calendar, projects, brain capture, and focus
Added Firebase-backed persistence with local/offline-friendly behavior
Created Tauri configuration for a native macOS version
Designed the Context Radar extension to suggest LifeOS activities from approved browser contexts

projects.title: National Park Matcher
projects.summary: Full-stack web application for discovering US National Parks through location-based search and trip planning
projects.tags: Python, Flask, HTML5/CSS3, Jinja2, REST APIs
projects.overview: Collaborative capstone project building a Flask-based web application for discovering US National Parks. Users can search 400+ parks by state or activity and get personalized driving directions.
projects.features:
Multi-criteria search supporting state-based and activity-based filtering
Real-time data integration with National Park Service API
Location-based Google Maps directions from user's starting point
Dynamic park detail pages with images and descriptions
Support for all 50 states and US territories
projects.contributionIntro: As the frontend developer, I was responsible for the user interface design and implementation:
projects.contributions:
Built main landing page with dual search functionality
Developed complete CSS styling system for consistent design
Created park listing and result pages
Collaborated on Flask routing integration

projects.title: Photo Sorter
projects.summary: Python CLI tool organizing images by EXIF metadata with automated audit reporting
projects.tags: Python, Pillow (PIL), EXIF Parsing, CSV, CLI
projects.overview: Command-line utility that automatically organizes photos into folders based on their EXIF metadata (date taken, camera model) and generates detailed CSV reports for auditing.
projects.features:
Automated sorting by date or camera model
EXIF metadata extraction from hundreds of images
CSV report generation for quality assurance
Batch processing of large photo libraries
Graceful error handling for corrupted files
projects.contributionIntro: Reduced manual photo organization time from 30 minutes to under 1 minute, saving approximately 90% of manual effort through automated EXIF parsing and intelligent file organization.

projects.title: Payroll Manager for HR
projects.summary: Bash automation system for payroll processing with tax calculations and searchable database
projects.overview: Interactive command-line tool that automates payroll calculations for HR departments, featuring time tracking, progressive tax calculations, and a searchable employee database.
projects.features:
Automated weekly timesheet processing
Progressive tax bracket logic (5%-15% based on hours worked)
Searchable employee database with fuzzy matching
Interactive CLI menu system with input validation
Monthly pay projections and tax withholding
projects.contributionIntro: Eliminated manual computation errors and reduced payroll processing time by an estimated 70% through automation of calculations, tax logic, and employee record management.

about.heading: About Me
about.intro:
I'm an IT professional transitioning into software development, with hands-on experience in system administration, technical support, and automation. I graduated from UMass Boston with a B.S. in Information Technology (Class of 2024) and have been building my development skills through personal projects and self-study.

My background in IT support has given me a strong foundation in troubleshooting, system infrastructure, and working with production environments. Now I'm channeling that experience into software development, focusing on automation tools, web applications, and scripting.

I'm particularly interested in roles that bridge IT operations and software development-like DevOps, infrastructure engineering, or backend development-where I can leverage both my operational experience and growing programming skills.

about.experience:
I've worked in IT support roles at Nexworks and Mass General Brigham, where I managed user accounts, deployed workstations, troubleshot technical issues, and improved documentation processes. These experiences taught me how to work under pressure, communicate technical concepts clearly, and solve problems methodically.

Outside of work, I build projects that automate repetitive tasks and solve real problems-like organizing photos, processing payroll, or helping people discover national parks. Each project is a chance to learn new technologies and improve my development skills.
about.education:

Relevant coursework: Cloud Integration Methodologies (AWS), Database Management, Systems Analysis & Design, Software Development, Networking
about.learning: Currently deepening my knowledge of data structures and algorithms, practicing problem-solving through coding challenges, and exploring backend development patterns. I'm committed to continuous learning and transitioning fully into software engineering.
